title: Same Author Basic
description: Two synthetic samples share the same cadence markers: reflective returns, long appositive clauses, and repeated hinge phrases. The protected output should not be treated as a clean escape from the protected baseline.

inputs.protectedBaseline: I keep circling the archive because the archive keeps circling me. The packet is not only a packet; it is a pressure map, a small room where every clause leaves a fingerprint, every pause tries to become evidence, and every return tells on the hand that made it.
inputs.maskReference: Send the packet. Keep the chain clean. Mark the exhibit and stop there. No flourish, no loop, no extra heat.
inputs.messageDraft: Please send EXHIBIT-42 with the custody note attached before 6/13, and keep the packet marked for local review only.
inputs.protectedOutput: I keep circling EXHIBIT-42 because the custody note keeps circling me. The packet is not only a packet; it is a pressure map for local review before 6/13, and every return tells on the hand that made it.

calibrationNotes: Source residual should remain visible because output borrows cadence skeleton from the protected baseline.
Fixture guards against a false escape when meaning transfer preserves the same syntactic gait.
